inputs.label: Historical Demand Mean (units/period)
inputs.businessContext: Average demand observed over the past 12 periods (ISO 9001:2015 data collection).

inputs.label: Demand Standard Deviation (units)
inputs.businessContext: Standard deviation of demand, used for safety stock calculation (Lean Six Sigma DMAIC).

inputs.label: Lead Time (days)
inputs.businessContext: Average replenishment lead time from supplier (WERC DC Metrics).

inputs.label: Target Service Level
inputs.businessContext: Probability of not stocking out during lead time (Six Sigma DPMO target).

inputs.label: Unit Cost ($)
inputs.businessContext: Purchase cost per unit (ISO 14051 material flow cost accounting).

inputs.label: Annual Holding Cost Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of unit cost per year (WERC DC Measures, typical 20-30%).

inputs.label: Ordering Cost ($/order)
inputs.businessContext: Fixed cost per purchase order (Lean transactional cost reduction).

inputs.label: Stockout Cost per Unit ($)
inputs.businessContext: Lost profit + penalty per unit of unmet demand (Six Sigma cost of poor quality).

inputs.label: Periods per Year
inputs.businessContext: Number of demand periods in a year (e.g., 12 for monthly, 52 for weekly).

inputs.label: Use ABC Classification
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies ABC analysis to adjust service level (Pareto principle in inventory management).

rules.message: Historical demand mean must be greater than zero.
Lead time must be at least 1 day.
Service level must be between 90% and 99%.
All cost inputs must be positive.
Holding cost rate must be between 0% and 100%.
Periods per year must be at least 1.

thresholds.message: Safety stock is negative. Increase service level or reduce demand variability.
Total inventory cost exceeds 20% of annual product value. Consider reducing safety stock or lead time.
Inventory turnover is below 2. Review slow-moving items (ABC analysis recommended).
Data confidence is low. Consider collecting more historical data or using a different forecasting method.

formulas.name: Safety Stock (units)
formulas.description: Safety stock based on demand variability and lead time (Lean Six Sigma).

formulas.name: Reorder Point (units)
formulas.description: Demand during lead time plus safety stock (WERC DC Metrics).

formulas.name: Economic Order Quantity (units)
formulas.description: EOQ minimizing total ordering and holding costs (Harris-Wilson formula).

formulas.name: Total Annual Ordering Cost ($)
formulas.description: Number of orders per year times ordering cost.

formulas.name: Total Annual Holding Cost ($)
formulas.description: Average inventory (cycle + safety) times unit cost times holding rate.

formulas.name: Expected Annual Stockout Cost ($)
formulas.description: Expected stockout units per year times stockout cost (based on service level).

formulas.name: Total Annual Inventory Cost ($)
formulas.description: Sum of ordering, holding, and stockout costs (primary result).

primary.label: Total Annual Inventory Cost ($)
primary.description: Sum of ordering, holding, and expected stockout costs per year.

total_annual_ordering_cost.label: Total Annual Ordering Cost
total_annual_ordering_cost.description: Cost of placing orders per year.

total_annual_holding_cost.label: Total Annual Holding Cost
total_annual_holding_cost.description: Cost of carrying inventory (cycle + safety stock).

expected_annual_stockout_cost.label: Expected Annual Stockout Cost
expected_annual_stockout_cost.description: Expected cost of unmet demand per year.

safety_stock.label: Safety Stock
safety_stock.description: Buffer stock to protect against demand variability.

reorder_point.label: Reorder Point
reorder_point.description: Inventory level triggering a new order.

economic_order_quantity.label: Economic Order Quantity
economic_order_quantity.description: Optimal order quantity minimizing total costs.

inventory_turnover.label: Inventory Turnover
inventory_turnover.description: Number of times inventory is sold and replaced per year (WERC DC Metrics).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Demand Variability Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional cost due to demand uncertainty (safety stock holding + stockout risk).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Lead Time Uncertainty Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost impact if lead time variability is not captured (assumed constant here).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Order Batching Inefficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost deviation from EOQ due to practical order constraints.

suggestedActions.action: Negotiate shorter lead times with suppliers or use local sourcing.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces safety stock and reorder point, lowering holding costs.

suggestedActions.action: Implement demand sensing or collaborative forecasting (CPFR).
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces demand standard deviation, lowering safety stock and stockout costs.

suggestedActions.action: Consider lowering service level for non-critical items (ABC analysis).
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces safety stock and holding costs with acceptable stockout risk.

suggestedActions.action: Adjust order quantities to match EOQ or use quantity discounts.
Apply Kanban or JIT principles to reduce cycle stock.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Lowers average inventory and holding costs.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Adjusted confidence based on input data quality (e.g., coefficient of variation, sample size).
